parsed_review.summary: CP00-166 is a descriptor-only Risk A pack binding CP00-165 matrix decisions to permission/audit decision outcomes for view/search/mutation/export_download/share/ai_retrieval across 10 units. The no-write and permission/audit boundaries hold: all constructors are pure frozen-descriptor builders, evaluates_runtime_permission/appends_audit_event/dispatches_review_route/dispatches_approval_route/executes_ai_retrieval are forced false, and no DB/API/network/UI/DOM/AI/analytics execution occurs. The special safety requirement is met: customer_facing_decision excludes permission_ref/audit_hint_ref/matched_rule_ref/matched_rule_id, which appear only in internal evidence descriptors; the exclusion is verified by computed checks over the actual object and asserted in tests. Contract, registry, validators, and validation script are mutually consistent (schema v0.11, current_pack CP00-166, 10 units, handoff to CP00-167/RP04.P06.M05.S08), and operator validation (1212/1212, 44/44, all validators, git diff --check) reportedly passed. Approved with three non-blocking P3 notes: plan/manifest not in diff, descriptor co-locates internal refs with the customer surface (selection-based guard), and catalog exercises only default scenarios.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-166 builds frozen permission/audit decision binding descriptors only; package code does not execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Coverage distribution depends on plan pack/manifest not present in diff
findings.description: validateMasterDataCp166Coverage hardcodes deliverable distribution (security_audit=2, test=2, implementation=6) and micro-phase distribution (RP04.P06.M04=3, RP04.P06.M05=7) read from planPack.included_units. The CP00-166 closeout-pack-plan entry and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-166/manifest.json are not in the reviewed diff, so the 10-unit plan binding and these distributions cannot be proven from the diff alone. The internal unit_scope (S18 fixture, S19 allowed, S20 denied, M05.S01 row, M05.S02-S07 actions) is self-consistent (10 units, M04=3/M05=7), and operator-supplied validation (rp04:master-data:validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, 44/44 model tests) reportedly passed.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the CP00-166 plan-pack entry and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-166/manifest.json are committed as pack evidence so the coverage distribution assertions are reproducible from the committed artifacts.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: CP00-166 manifest includes the full plan_binding_snapshot and all 10 included units; closeout-pack validation checks the manifest against the active plan.

findings.title: Descriptor co-locates internal refs with customer surface in one frozen object
findings.description: createMasterDataPermissionAuditDecisionBindingDescriptor returns matrix_decision (full CP00-165 decision containing permission_ref, audit_hint_fields, matched_rule_capture) and internal_evidence_binding (permission_ref, audit_hint_ref, matched_rule_ref) in the same frozen object as customer_facing_decision. The required separation is satisfied: customer_facing_decision is a separate flat object exposing only fixture_id/action/scenario/decision_outcome/safe_error_code/security_trimmed/review_required/approval_required, and customer_surface_excludes_internal_refs plus prohibited_output_absent are computed from the real object and asserted in tests. Boundary holds because renderable_surface is documented as customer_facing_permission_audit_outcome_only and nothing renders here, but the leak guard is by-selection rather than structural at the top-level object.
findings.recommendation: Keep the documented renderable_surface contract and ensure any future downstream renderer selects only customer_facing_decision; consider asserting downstream consumers never serialize the full descriptor to a customer surface.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: CP00-166 has no runtime renderer or customer exposure; downstream render/API packs must serialize only customer_facing_decision and never the full descriptor.

findings.title: Catalog exercises only the default scenario per action
findings.description: createMasterDataPermissionAuditDecisionBindingCatalog builds one row per action using defaultScenarioForCp166Action (view=allowed, search=allowed, mutation=review_required, export_download=denied, share=approval_required, ai_retrieval=ethical_wall). Several declared expected_scenarios (view object_acl, search cross_tenant, export_download legal_hold/approval_required, share legal_hold, ai_retrieval leak_prevention) are therefore not represented among catalog rows. The pack's stated allowed/denied coverage is satisfied (allowed_covered and denied_covered both true), and the descriptor function accepts arbitrary scenarios, so this is a completeness observation, not a boundary defect.
findings.recommendation: If exhaustive per-scenario binding evidence is later required, add catalog rows for the remaining expected_scenarios; otherwise document that catalog coverage is intentionally allowed/denied only.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: CP00-166 intentionally covers the planned allowed/denied test units and action binding rows; exhaustive per-scenario catalog expansion is reserved for later permission/audit binding packs.
